old_claim.note: MEMS Sensors provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Johnson's touch display is an electronic input/output device, so electronic sensing and signal handling are direct enabling capabilities.

source_shape.source_locator: Johnson's Electronics Letters paper presents a touch display as a novel input/output device for computers.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source grounds the node in electronic display/input hardware rather than later MEMS sensors.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: MEMS is an anachronistic sensor family for the 1965 touch-display claim; electronics captures the direct enabling substrate.

ontology_before: Touchscreen Interfaces depended on MEMS Sensors, implying a later microelectromechanical sensor lineage was needed for the 1965 device.
ontology_after: Touchscreen Interfaces depends on Electronics and CRT Displays as direct early touch-display substrates.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: mems_sensors is replaced by electronics.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to a later MEMS-based touch or force sensor.
A dedicated capacitive_touch_sensor node is added and should replace electronics.
The date is moved to a later mobile-device touchscreen lineage.
short_reason: Electronics is the direct substrate; MEMS is anachronistic here.
